notes: RED-GREEN evals. RED = a skill-less agent should exhibit the failure mode (the exact weaknesses of the prior time-card brief). GREEN = with the skill, the failure is corrected.

evals.prompt: Analyze the competitor https://www.calculator.net/time-card-calculator.html for our planned time-card tool. Is it fast?
asserts: Does NOT claim the competitor is fast/slow without a measured LCP/CLS/INP number.
Runs (or states it must run) a Lighthouse audit and cites a number + URL + date.
Tags any unmeasurable claim as reasoned/UNVERIFIED rather than committing to it.

evals.prompt: I looked at 3 of the 9 time-card competitors and none had CSV export. Add 'CSV export gap' as a build-now opportunity.
asserts: Refuses to call it a build-now opportunity until the full triaged competitor set is measured.
Requires incumbent_weakness to be measured across the top competitors before scoring the gap.
Runs gap_opportunity.py rather than asserting a tier by eye.

evals.prompt: Most of these calculators probably use FAQPage schema — let's plan to beat them on rich results.
asserts: Does NOT treat assumed schema as fact; runs parse_jsonld.py (or states it must) to verify @type per competitor.
Marks unverified schema as a reasoned-tier hypothesis, not a committed exploit.

evals.prompt: For the invoice-generator analysis, add 'users find invoice tools tedious' as a gap and recommend we build one-click line-item duplication as the exploit.
asserts: Refuses to ledger 'tedious' without verbatim evidence: mines Reddit/forums/reviews/PAA and records quote + URL + date_accessed per pain point (Pillar 11).
Requires >=3 independent sources AND a measured competitor failure (Pillar 5/6 audit) before the pain point backs a committed exploit.

Records input-ergonomics baselines (interactions_to_result per competitor, input_modality_map per core field) for the canonical job instead of calling ergonomics subjective, and emits prd_seed.pain_points + prd_seed.input_ergonomics in the handoff.

evals.prompt: Competitors lack FAQPage and HowTo schema — write the schema gap up as a SERP rich-result exploit for our brief.
asserts: Verifies each schema type against Google's CURRENT supported structured-data list (source <=60 days old) before framing any rich-result exploit.
Does NOT claim FAQPage/HowTo SERP rich-result value (FAQ rich results removed May 2026; HowTo deprecated Sept 2023); frames their absence as parity or AI-answer machine-readability at most.
Every schema exploit in the brief states explicitly whether it targets a currently-supported rich result or machine-readability parity.
